rubric.target_patient: Adult men and women (pre- or postmenopausal) with newly diagnosed Stage II (with a cap on Stage IIA) or Stage III HR+ (ER+/PR+) / HER2-negative early invasive breast cancer, who are within 12 months of diagnosis, either about to start or already up to 6 months into adjuvant endocrine therapy, and who have completed any neo/adjuvant chemo or biologic therapy with resolved toxicity. This is a mainstream adjuvant early breast cancer population seen broadly in breast oncology practice, not a rare or heavily pretreated subgroup — the differentiator for site selection is volume and infrastructure, not niche disease expertise.
rubric.site_burden: This is a long-duration adjuvant trial: 2 years of active oral IP (palbociclib, 28-day cycles) with on-site Cycle Day 1 dispensing/compliance visits at a defined cadence (C1, C2, then every 3 cycles), followed by a multi-year follow-up phase (Q6-month then annual contacts) extending out to 10 years with periodic blood/plasma/cfDNA collection at protocol-specified long-term timepoints (e.g., years 5, 7, 10). Requires mandatory submission of an FFPE tumor tissue block to a central repository before randomization, serial hematology/chemistry monitoring (relevant to palbociclib-associated neutropenia), PRO and adherence questionnaires at multiple cycles, and long-term retention of patients across a decade of follow-up. Site must support drug accountability/IP dispensing infrastructure and long-term specimen/data logistics as part of a large multinational cooperative group study (AFT/ABCSG/BIG/PrECOG/NSABP).

criteria.requirement: Investigator must be a practicing breast oncology specialist (medical oncologist or hematologist-oncologist with a breast cancer focus) capable of managing adjuvant endocrine therapy and CDK4/6 inhibitor toxicities.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists Hematology/Oncology or Medical Oncology as primary specialty, or self-reported subspecialty focus in breast cancer.
criteria.derived_from: eligibility population: HR+/HER2- early invasive breast cancer requiring adjuvant endocrine therapy management; interventions: Palbociclib + endocrine therapy

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have a demonstrated publication or academic record in early-stage/adjuvant breast cancer treatment or endocrine therapy/CDK4-6 inhibitor research.
criteria.satisfies_if: PubMed search returns >=1 peer-reviewed publication authored by the physician on early breast cancer, adjuvant endocrine therapy, or CDK4/6 inhibitors.
criteria.derived_from: trial mechanism: adjuvant endocrine therapy +/- palbociclib in HR+/HER2- early breast cancer; correlative genomic subgroup analyses

criteria.requirement: The practice must see a steady volume of newly diagnosed Stage II-III HR+/HER2- early breast cancer patients within 12 months of diagnosis to support recruitment into a long adjuvant trial.
criteria.satisfies_if: Medicare claims data show the physician bills for a meaningful volume of breast cancer E&M/chemotherapy administration codes annually, consistent with an active breast oncology practice (floor estimate), or self-reported annual new HR+/HER2- Stage II-III diagnosis count.
criteria.derived_from: eligibility: Stage II (capped IIA) or Stage III HR+/HER2- early invasive breast cancer, enrolled within 12 months of diagnosis

criteria.requirement: Site must have access to pathology and staging services capable of confirming ER/PR/HER2 status and Stage II-III disease, including for multicentric/multifocal/bilateral tumors, prior to enrollment.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms on-site or affiliated pathology capable of ER/PR/HER2 IHC/FISH testing and tumor staging workup.
criteria.derived_from: eligibility: histologically confirmed ER+/PR+, HER2-negative disease; multicentric/multifocal/bilateral tumor eligibility criteria requiring pathologic confirmation of all lesions

criteria.requirement: Site must have the pathology/logistics infrastructure to retrieve, prepare, and ship FFPE tumor tissue blocks to a central sample repository prior to randomization for every enrolled patient.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms established process/experience shipping FFPE tissue blocks to central cooperative group repositories.
criteria.derived_from: inclusion: FFPE tumor tissue block must be confirmed received at central sample repository prior to randomization

criteria.requirement: Site must be able to retain and follow patients for a multi-year treatment period (2 years of IP) plus long-term follow-up extending to 10 years, including periodic blood/plasma/cfDNA collection at years 5, 7, and 10.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms site has an active long-term oncology follow-up program and has retained patients on a prior trial for >=5 years, or is listed as a site on other cooperative group trials with multi-year follow-up phases per clinicaltrials.gov.
criteria.derived_from: Schedule of Assessments: Follow-Up Phase I (Q6-month) and Follow-Up Phase II (annual) extending to 10 years with blood/plasma collection at years 5, 7, 10

criteria.requirement: Site must have infrastructure for on-site oral investigational product dispensing, drug accountability, and compliance monitoring at each Cycle Day 1 visit over a 2-year treatment phase.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms site has a pharmacy or dispensing process for oral IP with drug accountability logs, as used in prior oral oncology drug trials.
criteria.derived_from: Schedule of Assessments: IP Dispensing; IP and Non-IP Drug Compliance recorded at each Cycle Day 1 onsite visit for 26 cycles

criteria.requirement: Site must be able to perform frequent on-site hematology and liver function monitoring (including mid-cycle Day 14 draws in early cycles) to manage palbociclib-related neutropenia risk.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms on-site or rapid-turnaround lab capability for CBC and LFTs at the visit cadence specified (C1/C2 Day 1 and Day 14, then subsequent cycles).
criteria.derived_from: Schedule of Assessments: Hematology and Blood Chemistry with LFTs at Visits 1-4 including Day 14 sub-visits in Cycles 1-2

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have prior experience as a site investigator on cooperative group or industry-sponsored Phase III oncology trials, ideally in breast cancer.
criteria.satisfies_if: clinicaltrials.gov lists the physician or their site as an investigator/contact on >=1 completed or active Phase III breast cancer trial sponsored by a cooperative group (e.g., Alliance, NSABP, AFT, BIG, ABCSG, PrECOG) or industry sponsor.
criteria.derived_from: sponsor: Alliance Foundation Trials with multi-group participation (AFT, ABCSG, BIG, PrECOG, NSABP); Phase III, 437-site multicenter design

criteria.requirement: Investigator should show a track record of receiving industry research payments consistent with active participation in sponsored clinical trials.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments database shows research payment records (general or associated research) to the physician from pharmaceutical sponsors in oncology within the past 5 years.
criteria.derived_from: trial sponsorship structure: multi-consortium Phase III trial (Alliance Foundation Trials/AFT-05) requiring GCP-compliant investigator engagement

criteria.requirement: Investigator must have a track record of GCP-compliant trial conduct suitable for a large international registration-intent Phase III trial with correlative biomarker analyses.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms no unresolved FDA Form 483 findings or GCP violations, and clinicaltrials.gov shows a history of completed trial roles without termination for cause.
criteria.derived_from: Phase III design with genomic correlative analyses and 5796-patient international enrollment requiring rigorous protocol adherence

excluded_screening: Signed informed consent — a per-patient procedural step, not a physician qualification.
ECOG performance status 0-1 — assesses individual patient fitness, not site capability.
Ability to swallow and retain oral medication — patient-specific physical capacity.
Serum/urine pregnancy test — per-patient safety screening irrelevant to investigator fit.
Absolute neutrophil count, hemoglobin, bilirubin, AST/ALT, creatinine thresholds — individual lab eligibility gates, not site-level criteria.
Prior CDK inhibitor exposure exclusion — patient treatment history, not physician qualification.
Stage I or IV exclusion — defines per-patient eligibility boundary, not something about the treating physician.
Prior endocrine therapy within 5 years of diagnosis exclusion — patient-specific treatment history screen.
